name: Pozo Airón Trail
name_original: Embalse del Molinillo desde La Almarcha – circular por sendero del Pozo Airón (PR-CU 122)
description: Hike the moderate 8.2-mile Pozo Airón Trail to experience a unique waterfall, agricultural landscapes, and views of the Duero River canyon.
region_intro: Hiking around La Almarcha offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and open countryside. The region features trails that connect to points of interest like historical castles and natural springs. Paths often follow gentle gradients, suitable for exploring the local environment. Best hiking trails around La Almarcha The most popular hiking route is Mirror of Valverde Trail (PR-CU 70), a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the local countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Cuerdas-Castillo de Garcimuñoz Trail, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain, connecting to the historic Castillo de Garcimuñoz. Local hikers also love the Pozo Airón Trail, a 8.2 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through open landscapes and past the Pozo Airón, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around La Almarcha is defined by rolling hills, open fields, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in La Almarcha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from 5 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore La Almarcha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Almarcha?
A: There are 10 hiking trails recorded in the La Almarcha area on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through rolling hills and open countryside.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, La Almarcha offers 3 easy hiking routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013187620/mirror-of-valverde-trail-pr-cu-70" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror of Valverde Trail (PR-CU 70)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.5 miles (5.6 km) long and takes about 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, providing gentle views of the local landscape.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of hikes in La Almarcha?
A: Hikes in La Almarcha vary in length, from shorter routes like the 3.5-mile (5.6 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013187620/mirror-of-valverde-trail-pr-cu-70" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror of Valverde Trail (PR-CU 70)&lt;/a&gt;, to longer excursions such as the 11.1-mile (17.9 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013200639/castillo-de-haro-circular-from-villar-de-la-encina-by-the-camino-de-la-virgen-de-la-cuesta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Haro – circular from Villar de la Encina by the camino de la Virgen de la Cuesta&lt;/a&gt;, which can take around 4 hours 48 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes in La Almarcha are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013200639/castillo-de-haro-circular-from-villar-de-la-encina-by-the-camino-de-la-virgen-de-la-cuesta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Haro – circular from Villar de la Encina by the camino de la Virgen de la Cuesta&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013721830/castillo-de-garcimunoz-from-la-almarcha-circular-by-pozo-airon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz from La Almarcha – circular by Pozo Airón&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What kind of historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: The trails often connect to historical points of interest. You can explore sites like the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013207276/las-cuerdas-castillo-de-garcimunoz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cuerdas-Castillo de Garcimuñoz Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villaescusa de Haro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Almarcha?
A: La Almarcha's landscape of rolling hills and open fields is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while winter can provide a different, quieter experience. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are often preferred.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Almarcha dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the La Almarcha region are suitable for walking with dogs, especially those through open countryside. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Almarcha?
A: The komoot community rates the trails in La Almarcha highly, with an average score of 3.4 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the region's tranquil open landscapes and the opportunity to discover historical sites along the way.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to natural springs or unique natural features?
A: Yes, the region is known for natural springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013720676/pozo-airon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Airón Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through open landscapes and past the Pozo Airón, offering a chance to experience one of the area's natural features.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes?
A: While La Almarcha primarily features easy to moderate trails, some routes offer a longer distance and more elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013200639/castillo-de-haro-circular-from-villar-de-la-encina-by-the-camino-de-la-virgen-de-la-cuesta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Haro – circular from Villar de la Encina by the camino de la Virgen de la Cuesta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.1-mile (17.9 km) route with over 700 feet (224 m) of ascent, providing a more extended outing.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in La Almarcha during autumn?
A: During autumn, layers are recommended for hiking in La Almarcha. Mornings can be cool, while midday temperatures are often pleasant. Opt for breathable clothing, a light jacket, and comfortable, sturdy hiking shoes suitable for varied terrain, including dirt paths and potentially rocky sections.
tips: Not very well known, the so-called "airones wells" are pagan elements that were believed to be a bridge to the underworld. In particular, the La Almarcha well was once believed to be the gateway to hell and even a connection to the distant Mediterranean Sea, being visited on several occasions by Emperor Carlos I. Today you may or may not continue to believe in these hypotheses, The truth is that you can visit it by following a picturesque path through this area of the interior of La Mancha.

Located at the foot of La Sarten hill and very close to the town of La Almarcha, the Molinillo reservoir is a beautiful and quiet place that offers very interesting views of this area of the interior of La Mancha.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Pozo Airón Trail?
faqs.answer: The ideal seasons to hike this trail are autumn, winter, and spring. During these times, the waterfall is more impressive due to abundant rainfall. Summer can be very hot, and there are no potable water sources along the route, making it less comfortable for hiking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail often starts on a wide dirt track, passing through orchards, vineyards, and olive groves. As you approach the waterfall, the path narrows and follows the Arroyo de los Cuernos, featuring small ascents and descents through a modest granite canyon. The final approach to the Pozo Airón involves a steep descent, where you should be careful of potentially wet and slippery rocks.

faqs.question: Is the Pozo Airón Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally rated as easy to medium difficulty, making it suitable for various fitness levels and families. However, sure-footedness and sturdy shoes are recommended, especially for the steep descent to the Pozo Airón waterfall, where rocks can be wet and slippery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Pozo Airón waterfall itself, a beautiful 20-meter cascade where you can uniquely walk behind the curtain of water. You'll also see traditional agricultural landscapes, Mediterranean vegetation, and potentially panoramic views of the Duero River canyon. The Molinillo Reservoir is another notable feature on this route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pozo Airón Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural park areas and near the steep sections around the waterfall, for their safety and to protect local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pozo Airón Trail. It is freely accessible to the public within the Arribes del Duero Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While this specific Komoot tour starts from La Almarcha, a common access point for the Pozo Airón waterfall is from Pereña de la Ribera. You can drive part of the way to a designated parking area located approximately 1.5 km from the waterfall, which can shorten the hike.

faqs.question: What makes the Pozo Airón waterfall unique?
faqs.answer: The Pozo Airón waterfall offers a unique experience because of a natural passage or cave behind the curtain of water. This allows hikers to walk through and view the waterfall from within, providing a distinct and immersive perspective.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route largely follows the Sendero del Pozo Airón (69% of the route). It also briefly touches sections of the Camino Honrubia (5%), Puente de la Calzada (4%), and Puente de la Vega (3%).

faqs.question: Is there potable water available along the route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no potable water sources directly along the Pozo Airón Trail. It is essential to carry enough water with you, especially if hiking during warmer months.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potentially wet sections, sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are highly recommended. Bring sufficient water, especially in summer. Layers of clothing are advisable for changing weather, and a camera to capture the scenic views and the unique waterfall experience.
